title: centaurea boissieri subsp. beltranii
intro: Centaurea boissieri subsp. beltranii, also known as Beltran's knapweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, specifically native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds. The health benefits associated with Centaurea boissieri subsp. beltranii include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cognitive function, and support immune system health. In herbal preparations, the plant's flowers, leaves, and roots are used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be used to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and respiratory problems.
